description: A dashboard for VPN infrastructure composed of multiple OpenWRT devices.

panels.title: mwan3 Online State
panels.description: One lane per mwan3 interface — red when mwan3 considers it offline, green when online.

panels.title: Failovers (24h)
panels.description: mwan3 interface up/down transitions in the last 24h.

panels.title: Active seconds — wan
panels.description: Cumulative seconds mwan3 has tracked the cable WAN as up. Resets on exporter restart.

panels.title: mwan3 Tracking Score (0–10)
panels.description: Rolling mwan3 health score. Drops when ICMP tracking probes fail; triggers failover at configured threshold.

panels.title: mwan3 Lost Tracking Pings / sec
panels.description: Rate of tracking pings that didn't return, per interface. Sustained >0 on the active WAN predicts a failover.
Number of clients connected per AP/BSSID as seen by DAWN
panels.title: DAWN: Clients per AP

panels.description: Channel utilization per AP as reported by DAWN (0-1 ratio)
panels.title: DAWN: Channel Utilization

panels.description: Signal strength of each connected client as seen by DAWN. Lower is worse.
panels.title: DAWN: Client Signal Strength ($node)

panels.description: Current DAWN client signal per AP — useful for comparing which AP sees a client better
panels.title: DAWN: Current Client Signal Snapshot

panels.description: Total DAWN-managed clients across all APs over time
panels.title: DAWN: Total Client Distribution

panels.description: Per-client signal strength from wifi_stations exporter
panels.title: WiFi Station Signal ($node)

panels.description: Busy state of all CPU cores together

targets.expr: (count by (instance)(count by (cpu,instance)(node_cpu_seconds_total{instance=~"$node",job=~"$job"})) - avg by (instance)(sum by (mode,instance)(irate(node_cpu_seconds_total{mode='idle',instance=~"$node",job=~"$job"}[5m])))) / count by (instance)(count by (cpu,instance)(node_cpu_seconds_total{instance=~"$node",job=~"$job"})) * 100

panels.title: CPU Busy

title: OpenWRT + mwan3
